scriptId: cs_v2_OVERRIDE_L40_Saving money for a specific goal
historyId: h_v2_OVERRIDE_L40_Saving money for a specific goal

goal: Down payment for condo: $24,000 in ~24 months.
constraints: Prioritize paying down high-interest credit-card balances before increasing down payment contributions if the card APR exceeds the expected HYSA return (roughly 4–5%).
decisions: Adopt a three-bucket setup: Down Payment (primary), Closing Costs, Moving; closing costs target $2,000–$3,000; moving target $500–$1,000; automate transfers to buckets and allocate windfalls to closing/moving first, then down payment.

Cadence plan: Monthly light operational review; Quarterly strategic review; 6 months before target increase focus on liquidity and closings; 3 months before closing detailed checks; 1 month before closing finalize funds.
entities: Bucket 1 — Down Payment (primary): HYSA or laddered short-term T-bills; automate bulk monthly savings ($1,000).
Bucket 2 — Closing Costs (small, liquid): Target $2,000–$3,000; automate a small monthly transfer; keep fully liquid.
Bucket 3 — Moving/Set‑up (very small): Target $500–$1,000; fund with windfalls or small monthly transfers.
open: Decide exact closing costs target range (2k–3k) and moving target (500–1k) and implement the three-bucket setup.
Set up automation for three buckets after payday; route windfalls to closing/moving first, then down payment.
Format checkpoints into a one-page calendar or reminder list you can plug into your phone/calendar.
